findings.description: The automatic CLI fallback (R3) provides zero resilience against synthesizer bugs like the line_range string-type crash (R3.S1), as both paths execute the same buggy logic. The proposal incorrectly frames this as an automatic recovery mechanism for such crashes.
findings.resolution: Clarify in R3 that automatic recovery is for environment/orchestration failures, while for shared-logic bugs the primary benefit is the durable checkpoint for manual remediation.

findings.description: Placing checkpoint_findings.py in skills/autopilot/ while having parallel-infrastructure/review_dispatcher.py import it creates a bidirectional dependency cycle, as autopilot already depends on parallel-infrastructure.
findings.resolution: Relocate checkpoint_findings.py to skills/parallel-infrastructure/scripts/ or a shared/ directory to maintain a clean one-way dependency graph.

findings.description: Design D6 suggests in-process callers write 'dispatches: []', but converge() has access to ReviewResult metadata. Omitting this information in the manifest reduces parity with the CLI dispatcher path and limits forensic usefulness.
findings.resolution: Update Task 4.2 to pass model_used, elapsed_seconds, and error_class from ReviewResult objects to the write_manifest helper.

findings.description: load_findings_from_dir (Task 2.2) lacks a fallback for manifests produced by legacy dispatchers that lack the 'vendors' key. This will cause the new synthesizer to fail when reading existing cache directories.
findings.resolution: Add a backward-compatibility fallback in load_findings_from_dir to support globbing findings-*.json if the 'vendors' key is missing in the manifest.

findings.description: The manifest atomic rename in D6 specifies fsync on the file but not the parent directory. Without directory-level fsync, the new directory entry may be lost during a crash, compromising the durability goals of the proposal.
findings.resolution: Add a requirement to fsync the parent directory after manifest rename and per-vendor file writes in checkpoint_findings.py.

findings.description: Task 7.1 integration test stubs the CLI to succeed for a shared-logic bug (line_range), creating a false proof of recovery. If the CLI shares the same buggy code, it will fail identically to the in-process synthesizer.
findings.resolution: Update Task 7.1 to verify findings durability on disk and the audit trail for double-failure, rather than stubbing an impossible successful recovery for shared bugs.
